turnaround_reason: SPS Commerce, a steady supply-chain SaaS compounder, lost over 60% from its high on growth deceleration concerns and the broader SaaS de-rating. With 6 consecutive EPS beats and net insider buying of ~446K shares, the basing setup looks like a healthy reset of a quality business.
bullish_points: Six consecutive EPS beats including +24% and +13% recent surprises
Net insider purchases of 446K shares in last 6m (15 buys vs 8 sells) — real conviction
Profitable (12% net margin), FCF positive ($152M FCF on $762M revenue = 20% FCF margin)
Clean balance sheet: $154M cash vs $6.6M debt; current ratio 2.1x
Forward P/E 11.3x is exceptionally cheap for a profitable SaaS with 5.8% revenue growth
11 analysts with mean target $68 (+15% upside) and high $103
bearish_points: Revenue growth has decelerated to 5.8% from historical low-double-digits
P/B of 2.3x and EV/Revenue of 2.5x not screamingly cheap on those metrics
Still -27% below the 200-day MA — long downtrend not yet broken
Insider 'purchases' include conversions/exercises — not all open-market conviction
Sponsored by retail-EDI demand which faces secular shifts
catalyst: Q2 2026 earnings demonstrating revenue re-acceleration or stable growth — would re-rate the multiple
suggested_trade: Spot position; or Jan 2027 $60 calls for leveraged upside on multiple re-rating
key_risk: Revenue growth decelerates below 5% next print, killing the 'quality compounder at a discount' thesis
would_skip_if: Q2 revenue growth comes in below 5% or guidance is reduced; insider buying turns to selling
